shot.subject: web
shot.notes: dawn: a finished web strung with dew, every strand jeweled and lethal

segments.text: Twenty-one episodes ago, this series promised that in the garden, everything is somebody's meal. This hour, we keep that promise — at the one address where the garden's stories collide.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: the corridor between two great trunks, webs spanning it like rigged sails

segments.text: A corridor of air. A net across it. And on every side, citizens who must fly through.

shot.subject: spider
shot.notes: the builder at her hub, still as punctuation

segments.text: This is the Web of Danger.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: glide slowly along the web's plane, the meadow refracted behind the silk

segments.text: Consider what this net actually intersects. The fireflies' night boulevard runs through here. The ladybugs' commuting lanes between floor and canopy run through here. This is not a trap placed in a wilderness. It is a toll booth built on a highway interchange.

shot.subject: firefly
shot.notes: night traffic: lanterns drifting toward the treeline corridor

segments.text: The traffic never learns, because the traffic never survives its own lessons. Each generation of fliers meets the silk with the same innocence — and the silk meets each generation with the same patience.

shot.subject: spider
shot.notes: macro on the forelegs resting against the hub's strands, reading

segments.text: She, meanwhile, has turned waiting into an information system. Every strand is a nerve she borrows; the whole web is one vast fingertip. She does not watch the corridor. She feels it.

shot.subject: firefly
shot.notes: a lantern weaves between the trunks, its glow brushing the darkness

segments.text: Night. A firefly works its slow signal down the corridor — flash, drift, flash — an advertisement that cannot be turned off.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: the strike: silk snaps taut; the light lurches, tilts, and jerks in place

segments.text: And then the corridor ends early. The glow that meant I am here now means it to exactly the wrong audience. Every struggle rings the web like a bell, and the bell has an owner.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: her silhouette crosses the net toward the trembling light; the glow dims to nothing

segments.text: We saw this moment from the firefly's side in episode two, and from hers in episode five. Seen whole, it is neither tragedy nor villainy. It is two honest strategies — advertisement and interception — meeting at the only place they could.

shot.subject: ladybug
shot.notes: morning: a ladybug whirs off a trunk, armored and unbothered

segments.text: Day shift. The ladybug's famous armor has bluffed the birds for twenty-two episodes — but the silk has never once asked what she tastes like.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: the impact: red shell against white net, wing covers pinned mid-open

segments.text: Glue does not read warning labels. The bright shell that shouted don't eat me to every eye in the garden says nothing at all to a thing without eyes. Her one perfect defense meets the one hunter it was never designed for.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: from beneath: the snared shell in silhouette, the owner already en route

segments.text: The web does not discriminate, and that is the whole terror and the whole fairness of it. Lantern or armor, night or day — the net subtracts whoever touches it, and the corridor's traffic flows on around the loss.

shot.subject: butterfly
shot.notes: a butterfly's tumbling path carries it straight toward a web — and past it

segments.text: For every capture the corridor records, it logs a hundred escapes — and the escapes are the better television. Watch a butterfly's drunken tumble carry it toward the silk, and then, by nothing resembling intention, past it. The evasive flight that evolved against birds pays an accidental dividend here: a flier that cannot hold a straight line is very hard for a stationary trap to intercept.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: a glancing strike: one wingtip brushes a strand, tears free, the flier lurches away

segments.text: Some escapes are closer than that. A wingtip grazes the spiral; the strand stretches, claims its toll of wing-scales, and lets go. The flier lurches off, lighter by a little dust, and the web keeps the fee — a near-miss economy running all day beneath the fatal one. Touch the silk and survive, and you have paid the corridor's cheapest possible price.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: a gust bellies the net; debris and one struggling captive shake loose in the flex

segments.text: And sometimes the corridor itself grants amnesty. A hard gust bellies the whole net, and what the silk held loosely — leaf litter, husks, now and then a living captive not yet secured — shakes free in the flex. The owner repairs and does not pursue. Chasing is not in her contract. The web catches, or it does not, and she has never once taken it personally.

shot.subject: web
shot.notes: beneath the net: the day's traffic passing safely on the far side of the silk

segments.text: So the corridor's full accounting is stranger than terror: most of the traffic passes, most of the touches tear free, and the net's actual harvest is the slim remainder — the unlucky percentile that keeps one spider fed. Danger in the garden is real, and it is also, on any given flight, unlikely. That ratio is what lets the corridor stay crowded.

shot.subject: tree
shot.notes: rise from the web up through the canopy: the whole meadow beyond, teeming

segments.text: Now pull back, and count what this intersection actually does. Without the webs: more fireflies, more ladybugs — until the meadow's arithmetic breaks. The webs are a brake on abundance, and abundance without brakes is how gardens die.

shot.subject: field
shot.notes: fireflies over the grass at dusk, undiminished, uncountable

segments.text: And the populations hold. For every lantern the silk takes, the field lights a hundred more. The toll is real, and the traffic survives it — that is what a working ecosystem looks like from close up: loss, continuously absorbed.

shot.subject: spider
shot.notes: she repairs a torn sector, spinneret to strand, methodical

segments.text: She, for her part, mends the net and resumes the wait. No malice, no mercy, no memory. Just the intersection, open for business, tonight and every night.

shot.subject: shore
shot.notes: from the treeline down to the waterline, where the ground dampens and darkens

segments.text: The web is the garden's driest danger. Its wettest is next — a hazard that does not wait in corridors but arrives, on schedule, over the border itself.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: a fish rising toward the flooded margin, silhouetted against drowned grass

segments.text: When the waters rise, the map becomes a menu. Next: When the Waters Rise.
